feed.title: SAP Community - SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
feed.subtitle: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How can i restrict access to custom CDS View Data by Access Control on aspect pfcg_auth in S4HPC
entry.content:
Hi.

I need to restrict access to CDS View Data by Access Control on PFCG in S4HPC.

How can i link Authorization Object with Business Role?

I've created custom CDS View

Also Access Control on PFCG authorization

I also created custom Authorization Object / Field ZVHAOBJ1 / ZVHFLD1. 

Now question: how can i assign Authorization Object ZVHAOBJ1 to particular Business Role to restrict access to CDS View Data for:

  • Users that has proper authorization can view data according to restriction described above (like, only particular suppliers and only if has Read authorization)
  • Other users cannot read the data

Regards

Vitali

entry.title: INVENTORY VALUTION AT COMPANY CODE LEVEL
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

How to set Inventory valution at Company code level in Public cloud.Add to that in my client there are 10 company codes, i need to set Company code valution for only 1 company code.

Best Regards,

Dinesh

entry.title: SAP GROW - Define Access Sequences & Maintain Condition Tables - Create
entry.content:
¡Hola a todos!
Espero que estén bien.

Estaba trabajando en la configuración de las secuencias de acceso a precios y las tablas de acceso relacionadas para la gestión de precios de venta en SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Implementación de inquilino CBC - BCS 100).

Después de crear las secuencias de acceso y las tablas, el sistema muestra el siguiente mensaje cuando intenta continuar.

Según mi análisis, la forma habitual de mover este tipo de contenido sería mediante la  aplicación "Exportar Colección de Software"

. Sin embargo, al ejecutarla, aparece un mensaje de advertencia adicional.

Luego intenté incluir los objetos relevantes en una colección de software para poder exportarlos.

Luego de realizar el paso de verificación la opción Exportar  permanece deshabilitada y no es posible completar el proceso.
Dada esta situación, me gustaría confirmar si este comportamiento es previsible en SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition y si existe una alternativa o un enfoque recomendado para gestionar la configuración de la secuencia de acceso a precios entre inquilinos. Agradeceríamos la orientación o el apoyo de SAP para aclarar el procedimiento correcto en este caso. Adjunto las capturas de pantalla correspondientes.

Muchas gracias por su apoyo.

Atentamente,

entry.title: WM Monitor queues WMQF* state SYSFAIL with no information abaout the reason
entry.content:
We are using SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud integrated EWM, doing usage decisions for inspection lots with follow-up actions to post quality stock to unrestriceted use stock.

 

At the WM Monitor (/SCWM/MON) queue entries 'Asynchronous execution of Follow Up Action with Retry logic' appear again and again.

First with the message text ' Command to tRFC/qRFC: Execute LUW again', later 'Max number of attempts of RETRY reached'.

When calling the application log, it appears: No display program registered for this queue - Message no. /SCWM/MONITOR055

How can I find out what is causing the faulty queue?

entry.title: Item text is missing in confirmation of requisition
entry.content:
Previously in the items data, business user can still add item text on it but now there is no field for it when we edit it to confirm/create the goods receipt for the PR/PO

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA PUBLIC CLOUD EDITITION - CONTENT UPGRADE
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

According to the relevant SAP information, my understanding is that after four weeks of the software upgrade, SAP will release the new version of the content upgrade.

  1. Can I understand that the content upgrade contains business process content, meaning that it automatically generates transport requests (TRs) related to business processes, which the functional team will then take over? (Does the content upgrade only contain TRs related to business processes?)

  2. Does the content upgrade have a version? For the next release, is the version of the Content Upgrade 2602?

  3. When SAP upgrades the software version for each system, does it also generate any TRs?

  4. The Hotfix Collection also generates TRs. Does it also have a version, and does it need to be imported into Q and P systems?

  5. Both Hotfix TRs and Content Upgrade TRs are named “BC Upgrade.” How can we differentiate between them?

entry.title: calculate using actual overhead costs in actual cost accounting
entry.content:
I want to calculate using actual overhead costs in actual cost accounting, but I don't know what settings to use.
In standard cost accounting, I have successfully calculated planned overhead costs.
Could you please advise me on the setup method and how to calculate actual overhead costs?

entry.title: Make field read-only in table but editable in Object Page
entry.content:
I have a custom app where in the Object Page, there is section for a table where i can add new business partner.

But I want the fields to be read-only when it is viewed as a table in the Object Page.

I want it to be editable when I access the row into another the BP Object Page.

I already tried using dynamic feature control, but the logic cannot tell whether the app is in table view or in object page view. Need your help on this matter. Thanks!

entry.title: Automatic Batch Determination in OBD for NLN Item Category – Unexpected System Behavior
entry.content:
STO is created manually in S/4HANA between a 3PL plant and an external plant.
After STO creation, the Outbound Delivery (OBD) is generated and re-routed to the 3PL via BTP.
Every process is executed in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Issue

In the Outbound Delivery, the system is automatically populating the batch.
This is not part of the intended design.

The requirement is that the 3PL should have the flexibility to select the batch on their own and perform PGI, and the selected batch details should then be updated back in S/4HANA.

 

As per standard SAP behavior, automatic batch determination happens only when the “Automatic Batch Determination” indicator is enabled for the OBD item category (in this case, NLN).

 

However, even after turning off the Automatic Batch Determination indicator for item category NLN, the system is still proposing a batch in the OBD.

Additionally, in some cases, the system is proposing an older batch with zero available inventory, which is causing further issues.

  • Why is the system still proposing batches in the OBD even after disabling Automatic Batch Determination for item category NLN?
  • Is there any other configuration or standard behavior that could be driving this batch determination?
  • How can we completely stop batch proposal in OBD so that batch selection is done only by the 3PL during PGI?

entry.title: Bank transactions going to wrong account -error "Account type K is not defined for document type DZ"
entry.content:
Hi all,

We have an issue with some bank transactions being auto allocated to the wrong customer accounts.

The logs in "Reprocess Bank Statement Items" show the error message "Account type K is not defined for document type DZ". The log does not mention any interpretation algorithms being used. We have also restricted the XBLNR number interval in Define Parameter sets.

Does this error message have anything to do with why these transactions are being auto allocated incorrectly and if so how do we fix it?

Thanks

entry.title: Replacing SMTP with Microsoft Graph API for Workflow Email Notifications in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have a requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to replace the standard SMTP-based email sending mechanism with Microsoft Graph API for sending email notifications from Procurement Workflows (PO status updates).

Currently:

  • We are using a standard flexible workflow for purchase orders.

  • Email notifications are sent via the standard SMTP configuration in Communication Arrangement.

  • These emails are triggered by workflow steps (approval, rejection, status update, etc.).

New requirement:

  • Due to corporate IT/security policy, SMTP usage is restricted.

  • We are required to use Microsoft Graph API (OAuth 2.0) for sending emails instead of SMTP.

We would like to understand:

  1. Is it technically possible in S/4HANA Public Cloud to replace standard SMTP with Microsoft Graph API for workflow emails?

  2. Is there any standard SAP-supported alternative to SMTP for outbound emails (for example, via SAP BTP services or API-based mail service)?

  3. Can workflow email notifications be redirected to:

    • SAP BTP (via API), and

    • then sent using the Microsoft Graph API from BTP?

  4. Are there any SAP Notes or official documentation confirming:

    • SMTP is mandatory for workflow emails, or

    • Is API-based email sending supported?

  5. Has anyone implemented a similar scenario using

    • SAP Build Process Automation / BTP Integration Suite / custom service with MS Graph API?

Our goal is to:

  • Avoid SMTP completely, and

  • Use Microsoft Graph API with OAuth authentication for all outbound workflow email notifications.

Any guidance on:

  • Supported architecture,

  • Limitations in S/4HANA Public Cloud,

  • or recommended best practice would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Harshal

entry.title: Exclude Billing Document from Scheduling Job if Allocation Number is not present
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community, 
We are looking for option to exclude the Billing documents that did not get 'Allocation Number' from E-Invoicing - Cockpit. 
Is there any BADI, Enhancement Spot or another system configuration to exclude the Billing document not to go Scheduled Billing Output if the Allocation Number is not present for that Billing document?
The process is: 
  1. Billing Document is created 
  2. Billing document goes to E-document (Job Run every 2 Min)
  3. Scheduled Billing Output (to Process Output To Complete)
Thank you in advance!

entry.title: #CoE CSRI How to manage data restore in public cloud?
entry.content:
Hi,

If there's data issue in PRD instance, can we request for database restore? What's the process? Thanks!

Eric

entry.title: Fetch data in Adobe forms using standard cds in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I my billing invoice form, I am trying to fetch additional data using custom logic implementation and custom fields. Currently I have exhausted the limit for creating custom fields.

I wanted to know if there is any alternate solution for this. Is it possible to fetch data from standard cds or standard API directly in my forms without using custom fields.
Is it possible to use scripts to fetch the data directly.

 

Regards,

Rakshna M

entry.title: Edocument cockpit for ksef
entry.content:
Hello experts,

As we have implemented ksef2.0 and tries to submit the document in Edocument cockpit Whereas it throws errors as it taxcodes are missing howeevr we havemaintained all taxcodes already .

Could someone suggest how can this recified.

entry.title: Functional Area Assignment Limitation at WBS Level During Enterprise Project Migration
entry.content:
Dear Expert.

I’m trying to mass-create Enterprise Projects using the Migration Your Data app.
However, it seems that Functional Area cannot be assigned at an individual WBS level.
When a Functional Area is entered at the Project level, it is propagated to all subordinate WBS elements.

In our case, Functional Area needs to be differentiated by the characteristics of each WBS under the same Project.

Is it correct that this functionality is not supported?
Is it not possible to assign Functional Area at the individual WBS level during migration?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: C_S4CFi- Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m preparing for the certification C_S4CFi –Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Financial Accounting , and I understand that the exam format has evolved recently.

For those who have taken it recently, could you please share your experience?

I’m especially interested in:

  • How the exam is structured (knowledge vs practical parts)

  • The level of difficulty compared to the learning journey content

  • The type of tasks or scenarios you encountered (process execution, configuration, troubleshooting, etc.)

  • How much hands-on system familiarity is really required

  • Any advice on what to focus on during preparation

Any feedback or tips would be greatly appreciated 
Thanks in advance for your help

entry.title: Additional Quantity 1 missing as Reference Key Figure in Top-Down Distribution
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I am working with the Manage Allocations app in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and configuring a Top-Down Distribution (TDD).

I need to use QUANT1 – Additional Quantity 1 as the Variable Portion Type in Receiver Basis.

The issue is the following:

  • When configuring Top-Down Distribution, in Receiver Basis → Variable Portion Type, the dimension QUANT1 – Additional Quantity 1 is not available for selection.

  • The additional quantity QUANT1 is already configured and active, and we are successfully using it as a reference in PA distributions, where it does appear correctly.

  • However, does not appear in Top-Down Distribution in the Manage Allocations app.

My question:

  • What should I do to make Additional Quantity 1 appear and be usable as a Variable Portion Type in TDD?

Any clarification or documentation reference would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: How to disable the Manage My Substitution option in the My Inbox App
entry.content:
Dear SAP Support,

we are encountering an issue regarding the My Inbox application in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud. Employees are currently able to maintain their own task substitutions using the "Manage My Substitution" function available in the user menu of the My Inbox app.

Problem Overview

The business requirement is that employees should not have the ability to maintain their own substitutions.

We attempted to disable the functionality via the related Business Catalog (SAP_CA_BC_MYINBOX_PC), but there is no corresponding app or configuration option available to deactivate the substitution feature.
We found the documentation here:
However, the instructions do not clarify how to disable the built‑in substitution feature, especially because the My Inbox tile already exists and does not allow for custom tile configuration.

Best regards,


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Billing remains possible, even though the Billing Block is set at the Sales Order header.
entry.content:
We have set the billing block on the sales order at header level but the invoice was still created. 

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.
